album.title: Sacred Geometry Vol. 1
album.label: Self-released
album.description: Ten compositions generated entirely from mathematical patterns — Fibonacci sequences, Mandelbrot sets, golden spirals, chaotic attractors, and the Thue-Morse sequence. Every note is deterministic, every structure traceable to its generating equation. No AI, no randomness, pure pattern.

tracks.title: Threshold
tracks.description: Fibonacci sequence mapped to pitch and rhythm. The simplest seed — where mathematics first becomes audible.

tracks.title: The Infinite Series
tracks.description: Per Nørgård's 1959 self-similar infinity series. Each interval contains the whole.

tracks.title: Golden Spiral
tracks.description: Phi-derived melodic contour. The climax lands precisely at the golden section.

tracks.title: Harmonic Series
tracks.description: The physical basis of consonance — overtones of a vibrating string become melody.

tracks.title: Logistic Map (r=3.7)
tracks.description: Edge-of-chaos dynamics. The same equation that models population growth creates a melodic line balanced between order and turbulence.

tracks.title: Mandelbrot Boundary
tracks.description: A walk along the boundary of the Mandelbrot set. Features the Goosebump Engine — a research-grounded appoggiatura at the golden-section climax.

tracks.title: Rössler's Strange Attractor
tracks.description: Otto Rössler's 1976 chaotic system. The attractor's suspended orbits create an unhurried, meditative quality.

tracks.title: Cantor's Dust
tracks.description: Fractal silence. Georg Cantor's middle-third removal creates characteristically sparse rhythms — what's absent shapes what remains.

tracks.title: Zipf's Law
tracks.description: The rank-frequency distribution found in natural language, city sizes, and music. A composition that follows the same power law as human speech.

tracks.title: Thue-Morse Resolution
tracks.description: The Thue-Morse sequence (1906) has zero autocorrelation — maximally avoiding self-similarity. After nine pieces built on self-similar structures, the final track lets mathematics refuse to repeat itself. The highest-scoring composition on the album.
